summary: Small header and struct-layout fixes made project headers usable as context for mips_to_c without local edits. The PR notes that mips_to_c's decompilation pass was stricter than MWCC, so it corrected C issues MWCC tolerated: adding an include guard to include/functions.h, giving ef_Spawn a named fixed first parameter before varargs, replacing sizeof(ftHit) in a Fighter filler-size expression with the literal 0x138, and adding the missing void return type to inline HSD_JObjSetScale.
what_changed: Added an include guard to include/functions.h using _functions_h_ and a closing #endif.
Changed the variadic declaration of ef_Spawn from void ef_Spawn(...); to void ef_Spawn(s32, ...); so the prototype has a fixed argument before the ellipsis.
Changed Fighter::filler_x1064 sizing from 0x1828 - 0x1064 - sizeof(ftHit) to 0x1828 - 0x1064 - 0x138 /* sizeof(ftHit) */.
Changed inline HSD_JObjSetScale(HSD_JObj* jobj, Vec* vec) to inline void HSD_JObjSetScale(HSD_JObj* jobj, Vec* vec), adding the explicit return type.
smart_moves: Fixed headers at the source instead of requiring mips_to_c users to maintain patched local context headers.

Preserved the meaning of the Fighter filler calculation by leaving a comment that 0x138 is sizeof(ftHit), while avoiding a construct that was problematic for the stricter decompilation context parser.
Made the variadic function prototype stricter and more standard by adding a fixed s32 parameter before ....
Added an explicit void return type to an inline header function, removing reliance on older C implicit-int behavior that MWCC may tolerate but other tooling rejects.
decomp_lessons: Headers used as mips_to_c context may need to be valid under stricter C parsing than the original MWCC build accepts.
Avoid implicit function return types in shared headers; specify void or the real return type so automated decompilation tools can parse them.
Variadic prototypes should include at least one fixed parameter when they are intended for modern or stricter C tooling.
For struct filler arrays, literal known sizes can be more robust for tooling than sizeof expressions, but comments should preserve the semantic link to the real type size.
Include guards matter for generated/decompilation context headers because context assembly may include broad headers multiple times.
naming_conventions: The include guard added to include/functions.h uses the lowercase filename-based form _functions_h_.
Existing project symbol casing was preserved: efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, ef_Spawn, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, and HSD_JObjSetScale.
Fighter struct filler naming retained offset-based names such as filler_xDF4 and filler_x1064, with field names such as x1064_thrownHitbox and x1828.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use headers as unmodified mips_to_c context by making them acceptable to stricter parsers.
When a type-size expression blocks tooling, replace it with the verified byte constant and annotate it with the original sizeof intent.
Tighten function prototypes in context headers to reduce parser ambiguity during decompilation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other headers with implicit return types that MWCC accepts but mips_to_c rejects.
Search for variadic prototypes declared only as ... without a fixed parameter.
Audit struct filler array sizes that use sizeof expressions and may confuse decompilation context tooling.
Check whether include/functions.h guard style _functions_h_ is consistent with nearby generated or aggregate headers.
